# Chugoku Region
Home to Hiroshima, Japan’s oldest shrine and gateway to the Seto InlandSea
Prefectures:
* [Tottori](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1231.html)
* [Okayama](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1232.html)
* [Shimane](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1237.html)
* [Hiroshima](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1238.html)
* [Yamaguchi](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1239.html)
Prefectures:
* [Tottori](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1231.html)
* [Okayama](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1232.html)
* [Shimane](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1237.html)
* [Hiroshima](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1238.html)
* [Yamaguchi](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1239.html)
The Chugoku Region (’†چ‘’n•û, Chūgoku Chihō, literally "central country") makes up the western part of Japan's main island Honshu. It is commonly subdivided into the heavily urbanized and industrialized **Sanyo Region** along the [Seto Inland Sea](https://www.japan-guide.com/e/e5445.html) coast and the much more rural **Sanin Region** along the Sea of Japan coast.
